codeReview.summary: The fix resolves prior no-op polling and WS/runtime wiring gaps, but a remaining event-ordering bug still causes incorrect live depth on full fills, so prior blocking correctness is not fully addressed.

issues.description: Events are polled and applied by type in fixed order, with `settlement::OrderFilled` listed before `settlement::TradeEvent` (lines 37-38). `OrderbookState.processOrderFilled()` deletes fully filled orders (api/src/state/orderbook-state.ts:401), while `processTrade()` only decrements depth if maker order still exists (api/src/state/orderbook-state.ts:344,350). In live polling, full-fill makers can be deleted before trade processing, so depth is not decremented/removed and orderbook deltas may be missed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill prescribes tests-first and manual runtime verification, but transcript shows implementation edits before new tests and no manual ws/curl runtime checks; handoff still reports followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows multiple Edit calls to api/src/state/orderbook-state.ts, api/src/indexer/event-indexer.ts, api/src/websocket/ws-server.ts, and api/src/ws.ts before Create calls for new test files; verification only lists automated pnpm test/typecheck commands.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared guidance does not explicitly document a required invariant for Aptos polling-by-event-type flows: cross-type events must be merged or handlers must be order-independent by transaction/version to avoid state drift.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Current implementation processes each event type in a separate loop (api/src/indexer/event-indexer.ts:137) and exhibits order-dependent behavior in state mutation (api/src/state/orderbook-state.ts:344-401).

summary: This fix successfully adds real RPC polling and WS-to-state wiring, addressing major parts of both prior failures. However, a blocking ordering defect remains in live event application (OrderFilled handled before TradeEvent), which can leave full-fill depth stale and prevents full closure of the original indexer/state correctness failure.
